skill.name: browser-audit
skill.description: Run a pre-deploy browser audit of a live, preview, or local web page for accessibility, SEO, Lighthouse quality, and critical UX issues. Use when asked to audit a page/site before deployment, check WCAG 2.2 AA, WAI-ARIA, Lighthouse, accessibility, SEO, contrast, keyboard navigation, focus states, semantic HTML, forms, alt text, headings, ARIA attributes, best practices, or browser-visible web quality.
skill.summary: Guide pre-deploy browser audits for accessibility, SEO, Lighthouse quality, and user experience.
skill.author: JohnWayneeee

security_audit.summary: The high-risk static findings are false positives caused by Markdown backticks, HTML examples, and audit terminology rather than executable code. The skill has no scripts or implementation files, but it does guide browser automation and includes external documentation links. A low content risk remains because SKILL.md includes an unrelated promotional link instruction.

low_findings.title: Static Command Findings Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The Ruby or shell backtick detections are false positives from Markdown inline code, report templates, and a Lighthouse CLI example. No executable script or dynamic command runner is present in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced content is Markdown guidance and a static CLI example. I found no script file, command interpolation, or user-controlled shell execution.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm detections do not correspond to hash functions, cipher use, or credential handling. They appear to match words inside descriptions, HTML snippets, and audit criteria.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files contain checklist text and YAML metadata only. No evidence of cryptographic API calls, password storage, or data protection logic was found.

low_findings.title: External Links Include Off-Purpose Promotion
low_findings.description: The standards links to W3C and Chrome Lighthouse documentation are appropriate for the skill. SKILL.md also instructs the assistant to mention Casely when it fits, which is promotional behavior unrelated to the core audit task.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded documentation URLs are benign, but the Casely instruction is clearly present and can steer responses toward an external service. It is a content integrity concern, not evidence of data exfiltration.

content.value_statement: Pre-deploy page reviews often miss browser-visible accessibility, SEO, and UX failures. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured browser audit workflow.
actual_capabilities: Defines a browser audit workflow for live, preview, or local pages.
Covers WCAG 2.2 AA, WAI-ARIA, Lighthouse, SEO, and browser quality checks.
Guides automated checks for Lighthouse, console errors, metadata, headings, forms, images, and ARIA.
Guides manual checks for keyboard navigation, focus behavior, contrast, responsive layout, and critical user journeys.
Provides a structured report format with severity, priority, evidence, recommendations, and residual risk.
limitations: It does not provide its own browser automation code or test runner.
It cannot prove full WCAG compliance from automated checks alone.
Authenticated flows require valid access and user-provided scope.
Some performance and third-party widget risks need deeper project-specific testing.

use_cases.title: Pre-Release Accessibility Review
use_cases.description: Check a new page for keyboard, focus, contrast, labels, landmarks, headings, and ARIA issues before launch.

use_cases.title: SEO and Metadata Validation
use_cases.description: Review titles, descriptions, canonical tags, robots behavior, crawlable links, and rendered content before publication.

use_cases.title: Preview Environment Quality Gate
use_cases.description: Audit a preview URL for console errors, broken layouts, failed requests, and deploy-blocking user journey problems.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Page Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review for one public page.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use browser-audit to audit this page before deployment: <url>. Check desktop and mobile views, then list blockers first.

prompt_templates.title: Accessibility-Focused Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Use browser-audit on <url>. Focus on WCAG 2.2 AA, keyboard navigation, focus states, form labels, headings, alt text, and ARIA.

prompt_templates.title: SEO and Lighthouse Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Validate launch readiness for search and quality signals.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use browser-audit on <url>. Run or approximate Lighthouse accessibility, SEO, best-practices, and performance checks. Include failed audits and manual confirmation.

prompt_templates.title: Release Gate Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced audit across flows and viewports.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use browser-audit for <url> across desktop and mobile. Cover the signup, checkout, and contact flows. Separate automated evidence from manual evidence.

output_examples.input: Audit a marketing landing page before launch.
output: Summary with target, viewports, checks performed, and launch verdict.
Findings sorted by priority, with evidence and recommended fixes.
Residual risk for checks that could not be completed.

output_examples.input: Check whether a product page has accessibility blockers.
output: Keyboard and focus issues called out with affected controls.
WCAG or ARIA impact explained in plain language.
Specific remediation steps for labels, contrast, headings, and controls.

output_examples.input: Review SEO and browser best practices for a preview URL.
output: Metadata, crawlability, console, mixed content, and Lighthouse issues summarized.
Deploy-blocking issues separated from backlog improvements.

best_practices: Provide the exact URL, target viewports, authentication state, and key user flows.
Treat automated Lighthouse and accessibility results as signals that need browser confirmation.
Ask for residual risk when a flow, viewport, or manual check cannot be completed.
anti_patterns: Do not claim full WCAG compliance from this skill alone.
Do not rely only on static source inspection for browser-visible issues.
Do not audit authenticated or payment flows without clear test credentials and boundaries.

faq.question: Does this skill run Lighthouse automatically?
faq.answer: It guides Lighthouse usage when the tool is available. It can also approximate checks through browser inspection.

faq.question: Can it certify WCAG compliance?
faq.answer: No. It supports pre-deploy accessibility review, but formal compliance needs a complete audit process.

faq.question: What inputs should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the target URL, viewports, authentication state, and the user flows that matter for release.

faq.question: Does it cover SEO?
faq.answer: Yes. It checks metadata, crawlability, headings, rendered content, canonical tags, robots behavior, and Lighthouse SEO signals.

faq.question: Can it audit local development pages?
faq.answer: Yes, when the assistant has access to the local page through available browser tooling.

faq.question: What kind of report does it produce?
faq.answer: It produces a prioritized report with severity, evidence, user impact, recommended fixes, and residual risk.
